evidence_summary: VN-05 controller marks the finite verb plus كُم object suffix as unsafe for infinitive prose and requires exact-address/two-vote review.
VN-05 controller marks the finite verb plus هُمْ object suffix as unsafe for root-family prose and requires exact-address/two-vote review.
VN-05 controller marks the fāʾ/future/verb/pronoun stack as component-only evidence that must not certify whole-token propagation.
VN-05 controller marks the article-bearing story noun as receiving unsafe verb-infinitive prose.
VN-05 controller marks the active-participle-looking nominal row as receiving unsafe verb-infinitive prose.
VN-05 controller marks the ر ج ل family row as component-only/context-sensitive and not safe for whole-token propagation.
VN-05 controller marks the dual body-part/relational noun with suffix as needing rich metadata and referent/context review.
VN-05 controller marks the plural body-part noun with هُمْ possessor as referent-sensitive and unsafe as an omnibus entry paragraph.
VN-05 controller marks the initial lām in لَّازِبٍ as lexical, not a preposition/proclitic; this is a false-clitic negative control.
